content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms like ICM can be reframed as a dynamic pruning and prioritization system for active learning agent memory, where only unexpected, unpredicted state transitions earn permanent storage in long-term context windows to address the 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of scaling agent memory without increasing context inference latency. This replaces the common fixed-size memory cutoff or recency-based retention with intrinsic curiosity as the core selection filter.
